differential_diagnoses: Nephrotic syndrome (secondary to IgA-dominant monoclonal gammopathy of renal significance)
Heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF)
Cirrhosis with portal hypertension (cryptogenic or related to underlying systemic disease)
next_steps: Perform 24-hour urine collection for total protein and creatinine clearance
Perform ultrasound-guided kidney biopsy with immunofluorescence and electron microscopy
Echocardiogram with strain imaging to assess for infiltrative cardiomyopathy (amyloidosis)
Cardiac MRI to further evaluate for constrictive pericarditis or infiltrative process
Bone marrow biopsy and aspirate to investigate monoclonal gammopathy
Serum albumin and lipid panel to assess the severity of nephrotic syndrome

differential_diagnoses: Monoclonal gammopathy of renal significance (MGRS) / Proliferative glomerulonephritis with monoclonal IgA deposits (PGNMID)
Heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF) exacerbation
Nephrotic syndrome due to primary glomerular disease (e.g., Membranous nephropathy)
next_steps: Quantify proteinuria with 24-hour urine collection or spot urine protein-to-creatinine ratio
Obtain serum albumin and lipid profile to confirm nephrotic syndrome
Perform renal biopsy with immunofluorescence and electron microscopy (Congo red staining is mandatory)
Perform echocardiogram with global longitudinal strain analysis for infiltrative patterns
Obtain cardiac MRI with T1 mapping and late gadolinium enhancement
Bone marrow biopsy with flow cytometry and cytogenetics to evaluate for plasma cell dyscrasia
Evaluate for systemic tissue deposits (e.g., abdominal fat pad biopsy if indicated)
